 $\boxed{Z(s)=\sqrt{\frac{R+sL}{G+sC}}\text{ Characteristic impedance of transmission line}}$
 Reflection coefficients
- output reflection coefficient
$N(s)=\frac{Z_{0}(s)-Z_{2}(s)}{Z_{0}(s)+Z_{2}(s)}$
- input reflection coefficient
$M(s)=\frac{Z_{0}(s)-Z_{1}(s)}{Z_{0}(s)+Z_{1}(s)}$
$Z_{2}$ output 
$Z_{1}$ input
Z line 

# lossless transmission line
## $R=G=0$

$Z(s)=\sqrt{ \frac{L}{C} }=p\text{ characteristic impedance of the line}$
$\gamma(s)=\sqrt{ (R+sL)(G+sC) }=\frac{s}{v}\text{ propegation constant}$
$v=\frac{1}{\sqrt{ LC }}\text{velocity of traveling wave} [\frac{m}{sek}]$

$r=\frac{l}{v}\text{ Transit time of the line [sec]}$

# Distortionless transmission line
## basic condition $\frac{R}{L}=\frac{G}{C}$

$Z(s)=\sqrt{ \frac{L}{C} }=p\text{ characteristic impedance}$

$\gamma(s)=\frac{s}{v}+\alpha\text{ propegation constant}$

$\alpha=\sqrt{ RG }\text{ attenuation factor}$

$v=\frac{1}{\sqrt{ LC }}\text{ velocity of traveling vale}\left[ \frac{m}{s} \right]$
propegation velocity

The same impedances = no reflections

for graphical negate coeficients for voltages
z

# part 2
data
$R_{1}=\frac{\rho}{3}$$R_{2}=3\rho$
$E=32V$ $\rho=75Ω$

$U_{0}=E·\frac{\rho}{\frac{\rho}{3}+\rho}=24V$
$I_{0}=\frac{\frac{E}{\rho}}{3}+\rho$

$N=\frac{\rho-3\rho}{\rho+3\rho}=-\frac{1}{2}$
$M=\frac{\rho-\frac{\rho}{3}}{\rho+\frac{\rho}{3}}=\frac{50}{100}=\frac{1}{2}$

 $U(t,l)=\frac{3}{2} U_{0}\mathbb{1}(t-\tau)-\frac{3}{8}U_{0}\mathbb{1}(t-3\tau)$

---

$R_{1}=R_{2}=\frac{\rho}{2}$
$U_{0}=\frac{2}{3}E$
$M=N=\frac{1}{3}$
$E=24V$
$U_{0}=\frac{\rho}{R_{1}+\rho}=\frac{2}{3}E=16V$
$M=\frac{1}{3}$
$N=\frac{1}{3}$

$U\left( t, \frac{l}{4} \right)=U_{0}1\left( t-\frac{\tau}{4} \right)-\frac{1}{3}U_{0}1\left( t-\frac{7}{4}\tau \right)+\frac{1}{9}U_{0}1\left( t-\frac{9}{4}\tau \right)$

---

$V_{0}=\frac{E}{2} \frac{\rho}{\rho+\rho}=\frac{E}{4}$
$M=0$
$N=$
 
 
 ---

Pulse generator count the number of people entering
$R_{1}=R_{2}=\frac{\rho}{2}$
$E=8.1V$
$\tau_{\omega}=2ms$
$l=400m$
$C=0.01 \frac{µF}{m}$
$L=0.01 \frac{H}{m}$

$Z(s)=\sqrt{\frac{L}{C}}=10^{3}Ω$
$M=N=\frac{1}{3}$
$V=\frac{1}{\sqrt{ LC }}$
$\tau=\frac{l}{V}=\frac{400}{10^{5}}$

$U_{0}=E \frac{\rho}{R_{1}+\rho}=\frac{2}{3}E=5.39V$
